text.title: Agentic AI Product Development — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every agentic-AI product spec must enforce. Replaces MVP with MVI (Minimum Viable Intelligence); the rules pin escalation-first design, machine-verifiable goal states, and behavioural regression.

rule.statement: Every agentic spec defines a machine-verifiable goal state (concrete predicate, not natural-language wish) BEFORE the happy path is written. "Solves the ticket" is rejected; "ticket.status == resolved AND customer_csat_response received" is accepted.

rationale.source: Anthropic Agent design patterns; 40% pilot-fail post-mortems

rule.rationale: Agentic systems need a machine-verifiable definition of success to know when to stop, escalate, or retry. Natural-language goals force rigid scripting and silent drift.

rule.statement: The spec writes the escalation path BEFORE the happy path: enumerate explicit triggers (confidence threshold, repeated-failure count, regulator-flag) and the named human role notified. Specs that defer edge cases to "v2" are rejected.

rationale.source: 40% of agentic pilots fail to productionize due to missing escalation; production incident post-mortems

rule.rationale: The happy path is optimistic; the escalation design prevents production disasters and is what stakeholders trust.

rule.statement: Every action the agent may take WITHOUT user approval is enumerated as a discrete action with its triggering condition. Free-form "the agent will handle complex cases" is rejected.

rationale.source: Contract-first product specs; behavioural-test driven development

rule.rationale: Unenumerated autonomous actions are unauditable; they cannot be tested, monitored, or rolled back.

rule.statement: Whenever the underlying model version changes (silent provider update OR explicit bump), the spec's goal-achievement-rate AND autonomy-ratio thresholds are re-validated on a held-out test set BEFORE traffic shifts. Auto-upgrade without regression is rejected.

rationale.source: Silent-update incident reports; behavioural drift literature

rule.rationale: Model providers update without code releases. Agentic behaviour can change silently; without regression tests, users detect incidents before operators do.

rule.statement: The spec's primary metrics are goal-achievement-rate, autonomy-ratio, and cost-per-task. Click-through, cost-per-user, or session-length are not accepted as primary agentic metrics because they measure the wrong scaling axis (seats vs task volume).

rationale.source: Agent unit-economics analyses; cost-per-task case studies

rule.rationale: Agentic systems scale with task volume, not seat count. Standard product metrics measure engagement; agentic metrics measure reliability and economic efficiency.

example.p: Customer-support agent spec: goal state = `ticket.status == resolved AND csat >= 4`; escalation triggers = `confidence < 0.7 OR retry_count > 2 OR refund > $100`; primary metrics = goal-achievement-rate 75%, autonomy-ratio 60%, cost-per-task $0.40.
"The agent will handle complex cases and escalate when needed." — no machine-verifiable goal, no enumerated trigger, no metric threshold.
